title: Keel Lorenzs nationality
wikitext:
[]: Keel Lorenz's nationality             Quote Post by Replicator » Wed Mar 06, 2013 9:36 am The EvaWiki says its Germany*, but I've done some original research of my own, and pinpointing an exact country might be difficult, but I have several theories. The surname Lorenz/Lorentz is derived from the Roman Laurentius , which means from Laurentum. Laurentius can also connotate "the one who wears the laurel wreath". This is significant in particular, because Keel is depicted as wearing a visor/headset and the "SEELE ritual robe", in addition to being the ringleader of SEELE. The name Lorenz, I've discovered can be German/Austrian or Dutch. Konrad Lorenz, Keel's prototype, is Austrian, and apparently Lorenz seems to be a fairly common Austrian surname. Could Keel actually be Austrian, as hinted at with his progenitor? Further, I know this is a stretch, but it is still possible that Keel's role in the Evangelion mythos may share parallels with the Nazi Austrian-German leader Adolf Hitler (albeit Keel isn't specifically a Nazi). Konrad Lorenz in real life was a member of the Nazi Party. The evidence for such an inference is thin, though, and I wouldn't consider this to be an "official" interpretation. As well, most of the other characters have Naval-related surnames. Akagi, Sohryu, Katsuragi, Zeppelin, Langley. Interestingly enough, Keel lacks one. It's stated through her surname that Kyoko Zeppelin Sohryu is half-German, while no sources make such a reference applying to Keel Lorenz. As well, the SEELE/Human Instrumentality Committee meetings probably take place in Tokyo-II, which was the new UN Headquarters after 2I. I remember seeing somewhere that Keel is also the Chairman of the UN, and in fact represents the UN on the Human Instrumentality Committee. While the Human Instrumentality Committee meetings happen through video conference with Gendo, it's possible they are on a relay from Matsushiro, where the UN (and possibly SEELE's) base of operations are. *Keel's actual page on EvaWiki doesn't list a nationality, but the Germany and Human Instrumentality Committee pages say its Germany. I'm only making my speculations here, and I hope I haven't opened a can of worms with this topic.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Wed Mar 06, 2013 11:15 am According to production notes, the character was actually going to be named Konrad Lorenz at one point. In as much as the tribute seems to have been quite literal, I think Austria is a fair assumption.

[]: Re: Keel Lorenz's nationality             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Mar 07, 2013 1:18 am Replicator wrote: As well, the SEELE/Human Instrumentality Committee meetings probably take place in Tokyo-II, which was the new UN Headquarters after 2I. I remember seeing somewhere that Keel is also the Chairman of the UN, and in fact represents the UN on the Human Instrumentality Committee. While the Human Instrumentality Committee meetings happen through video conference with Gendo, it's possible they are on a relay from Matsushiro, where the UN (and possibly SEELE's) base of operations are.

[]:             Quote Post by Maru » Sat Mar 09, 2013 3:45 am I must admit I had originally just assumed he was German. I will allow that he could be a German from Germany, Austria, Switzerland, or any number of countries (there are, or were, many ethnic Germans in Poland and the Czech Republic, and many German-speaking people in Alsace-Lorraine), and it is possible also that he could be from Netherlands, the Dutch-speaking parts of Belgium, and Denmark. Personally though, I still feel (and this is just my own theory) that he is most likely from Germany, or possibly from Denmark. The reason I say this is that his surname, "Kiel," is also the name of a German port city in Schleswig-Holstein, the northernmost state of Germany. It borders Denmark and touches both the North Sea and the Baltic Sea, and was at one time actually ruled by Denmark; it was won back by Prussia during the nineteenth century as part of Otto von Bismarck's project of German unification. It seems logical to me to assume that a man named Lorenz Kiel would either come from Kiel, or would have had antecedents who called that place home. It probably wasn't considered significant to the plot to flesh out where he was from or who he really was. While many fans have attempted to identify the Seele committee members by nationality, there is really no way that I can see to make such distinctions. None of the ones that we see in the holograms conform to any specific national stereotypes that I know of, except in the sense that they are all or nearly all stereotypically European: big noses, protuberant chins, prominent eyebrows, big mustaches, etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 09, 2013 5:26 am Maru : His name is Keel Lorenz, in that order. Also, most of committee's nationalities are identified in a production drawing. No guessing required.

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Thu Mar 28, 2013 6:37 pm Germany, Austria... same thing.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Mar 28, 2013 6:55 pm Um, no?

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:12 pm My world maps from 1938 tell a different story.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:15 pm This just in - it's not 1938 any more.

[]:             Quote Post by Elect G-Max » Thu Mar 28, 2013 7:17 pm pwhodges wrote: This just in - it's not 1938 any more.
